details.body: src/adapters/process_backend.rs:2463-2533 removed the `unify_single_enum_oneof` transform from `enforce_strict_mode_schema`. That function now only handles nullable normalization and single-element `allOf`, then recurses, so schemas can retain field-level `oneOf` single-element-enum patterns (`oneOf: [{enum:["A"], type:"string"}, ...]`) that OpenAI strict mode rejects for field/property schemas. This reintroduces a runtime schema-compatibility regression affecting structured-output calls; restore `oneOf` string-enum collapse here (or equivalent).

Non-fix classifications still trigger the normal fix-now path. `ReviewFindingClass::triggers_restart` returns `true` for every variant at src/contexts/workflow_composition/review_classification.rs:45-49, final_review uses that to request restarts at src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:2030-2032, and `partition_final_review_amendments_by_route` puts every accepted amendment into `restart_queue` while leaving the routed list empty at src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:9040-9051. This means `covered_by_existing_bead`, `propose_new_bead`, and `informational_only` findings still amend/restart the current bead instead of being routed or no-op as required.

`covered_by_existing_bead` findings are not reconciled into target-bead comments. The success reconciler only invokes legacy planned-elsewhere verification at src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:287-300, while aggregate reconstruction explicitly skips non-`FixCurrentBead` classifications at src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1267-1279. Even the legacy comment body at src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:969-980 lacks the required `amendment_id` marker and source run ID. Accepted covered-by findings therefore do not produce the required idempotent target-bead comment, and missing target beads are not handled on this path.

`propose_new_bead` reconciliation still auto-creates beads instead of threshold-gated NDJSON proposals. `reconcile_proposed_beads_after_success` iterates each proposed amendment and calls `milestone_service::handle_propose_new_bead` at src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1101-1145; that handler calls `br_mutation.create_bead` and adds a dependency at src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:5139-5179. There is no grouping by trimmed case-insensitive `proposed_bead_summary`, no idempotent append to `<project_root>/proposed-beads.ndjson`, and no `workflow.new_bead_proposal_threshold` setting in `WorkflowSettings` or known config keys at src/shared/domain.rs:1227-1248 and src/contexts/workspace_governance/config.rs:1712-1733.

Terminal failure does not run classification reconciliation. The required behavior applies after `RunCompleted` or `RunFailed`, but `reconcile_failure` only forwards failure metadata into `reconcile_failure_sync` at src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:107-156, and the sync path starts by reading milestone journal/run lineage for failure accounting at src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:181-205 with no scan of recorded final-review findings or covered/proposed side effects. A bead-backed run that ends in final failure leaves recorded classifications unreconciled.

The new `workflow.new_bead_proposal_threshold` setting does not exist in the effective config model or parser path. `WorkflowSettings` has no such field (`src/shared/domain.rs:1228-1248`), `known_config_keys()` does not expose `workflow.new_bead_proposal_threshold` (`src/contexts/workspace_governance/config.rs:1712-1754`), and `apply_to_document` has no setter branch for it (`src/contexts/workspace_governance/config.rs:1776-1988`). As a result, `rb config set/get` cannot accept/use this setting and thresholding cannot be configured.

There is no terminal replay path that reconciles `covered_by_existing_bead` findings into target-bead comments with amendment-id idempotency. `reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings` explicitly skips `covered_by_existing_bead` (except legacy planned-elsewhere) and logs it as deferred (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1268-1277`), while planned-elsewhere commenting uses a fixed text without amendment marker (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:970-972`). So covered-by findings never create a deduped comment on the target bead at RunCompleted.

`reconcile_proposed_beads_after_success` still executes proposals as follow-up actions instead of proposal handoff. It filters only `AmendmentClassification::ProposeNewBead`, builds creation input, and directly calls `milestone_service::handle_propose_new_bead` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1104-1105`, `1109-1114`, `1135-1145`), which mutates `.beads` and creates proposed beads rather than emitting `proposed-beads.ndjson` records.

Threshold and idempotency semantics for `ProposeNewBead` are not implemented. The loop processes each accepted amendment independently (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1037-1108`) with no grouping/counting by `proposed_bead_summary` (case-insensitive/trim-normalized), no `N`-of check, and no existing `proposed-beads.ndjson` read/write/dedupe via `amendment_id`. This prevents threshold gating and re-run idempotency.

src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1070 and :1084 restrict terminal reconciliation to the max completion_round `StageId::FinalReview` aggregate. This drops recorded non-fix findings from normal `review` stages, which the engine now explicitly defers without remediation at src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5473, and it also drops non-fix final-review findings from earlier completion rounds once a later round completes. The reconciler needs to walk all committed review/final_review findings for the run, with per-round latest aggregate selection where relevant, instead of only the latest final-review round.

src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1289 counts `amendments.len()` for the proposal threshold, but final-review aggregation canonicalizes duplicate reviewer findings into one `FinalReviewCanonicalAmendment` with multiple `sources`. With threshold=2, two reviewers can raise the same proposed_bead_summary and still produce a single canonical amendment, so no NDJSON record is written even though the same summary appeared in two findings. Count the underlying finding/source occurrences for each summary, not just canonical amendment rows.

src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1226 and :1242 swallow `br comments add` and `br sync --flush-only` failures with only warnings. The only specified skip path is a missing target bead; other mutation or flush failures leave the target bead without the required idempotent comment while terminal reconciliation still reports success and will not reliably retry. Return a reconciliation error for comment/sync failures after the target bead has been found.

`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1216` uses `comment.text.contains(&amendment.amendment_id)` when deduplicating covered-by reconciliation comments. This is substring-based and can suppress a legitimate new comment if an unrelated existing comment already contains that token, so an important target-bead linkage can be skipped on first post for the run.

src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1313-1318 and 1339-1344 can mark a covered_by_existing_bead comment as reconciled even after a prior sync failure. If the first attempt posts the comment locally and `sync_flush` fails, the failure-path retry from src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:2569-2578 sees the local `amendment_id` marker, skips posting, skips sync because `comments_posted == 0`, and returns Ok while the required comment may still be unflushed. Flush own pending mutations even when the marker already exists, preferably with the guarded own-dirty sync path.

`src/contexts/workflow_composition/payloads.rs:125` only treats `covered_by_bead_id` / `proposed_bead_summary` as missing when the option is `None`, so whitespace-only values keep the finding classified as `covered_by_existing_bead` or `propose_new_bead`. With `triggers_restart()` now false for those classes (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/review_classification.rs:45`), the engine suppresses immediate remediation, but terminal reconciliation later trims and skips the blank target/summary (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1281`, `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1219`). That can silently drop a review finding. Normalize required classification fields with `trim()` during `ClassifiedFindingWire` conversion or semantic validation, and downgrade/error before suppressing restart.

`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5473` handles non-fix `ReviewOutcome::RequestChanges` by logging only. After that arm, the generic cursor advancement still runs (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5653`), so quick-dev runs advance from `review` into `apply_fixes` even when all review findings were `covered_by_existing_bead`, `propose_new_bead`, or `informational_only`. That still routes non-fix feedback through the implementer/fix stage. When there are no restart-triggering follow-ups, skip `apply_fixes` or otherwise treat the review as pass-through for stage advancement.

The direct CLI terminal-sync path still bypasses the new terminal classification reconciliation. Failed runs call only `reconcile_failure_sync` and return at `src/cli/run.rs:1421`/`src/cli/run.rs:1442`, while completed runs directly update milestone/controller state at `src/cli/run.rs:1467` and `src/cli/run.rs:1503` without calling `reconcile_terminal_review_classifications` or performing the required `br update ... closed`. Because `run` and `resume` call this path before returning to the operator (`src/cli/run.rs:3746`, `src/cli/run.rs:4003`), operator-driven bead-backed runs will skip covered-by-existing comments and proposed-beads records, and success will not close the bead through the new reconciliation path.

High: In the CLI terminal path, `sync_terminal_milestone_task_and_continue_selection_with_terminal_reconciliation` first calls `sync_terminal_milestone_task_with_options` and only then closes the bead (`src/cli/run.rs:1002`, `src/cli/run.rs:1013`). For completed runs, that sync path records the bead task as succeeded and may advance the controller before `close_cli_terminal_bead` runs (`src/cli/run.rs:1758`, `src/cli/run.rs:1776`). If `br update --status=closed` or the follow-up sync fails, the command returns an error after milestone state already says the bead succeeded, leaving the bead open while the milestone has advanced. Close and flush the bead before recording success, matching the daemon success ordering.

Medium: `is_missing_bead_error` treats any `br show` exit output containing `not found` as a missing target bead (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1247`). The covered-by-existing path then logs and skips the required comment (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1299`). This can silently drop reconciliation when `br show` failed for a different reason such as a missing/corrupt bead database path. Restrict this to bead/issue-not-found messages or reuse the existing stricter missing-bead detector.

src/cli/run.rs:1002 closes the bead before sync_terminal_milestone_task_with_options has proven that the terminal attempt will be reconciled. That sync can still return Ok(false) for completed runs when lineage repair is disabled or blocked (src/cli/run.rs:1578-1591) or when no repairable lineage exists (src/cli/run.rs:1630-1635). In those cases the CLI can run br update --status=closed and then report no milestone sync, leaving a stale or unreconcilable project as a closed bead without a recorded success. Gate the close behind the same lineage validation path, while preserving close-before-success ordering once the attempt is known reconcileable.

src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:8210-8231 still backfills follow_up_or_amendments as legacy fix_current_bead items whenever the text is not an exact match for classified_findings. Because build_queued_amendments then queues only trigger-restart items at src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:8296-8299, a reviewer can classify a finding as covered_by_existing_bead or propose_new_bead but include a semantically identical, differently worded legacy follow-up, causing apply_fixes to run anyway. Make classified_findings authoritative when it is non-empty, or otherwise prevent the legacy fallback from reclassifying deferred findings as fix-current.

src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1327 posts the covered_by_existing_bead comment before checking whether the bead export is safe to mutate; the only health gate is sync_own_dirty_if_beads_healthy at src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1340-1342, after the mutation already happened. If .beads/issues.jsonl is conflicted or malformed, reconciliation can still call br comments add and only then refuse to flush. Add a pre-mutation beads-health guard before comment_bead, matching the existing close and planned-elsewhere comment safety pattern.

In `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs`, thresholding for `propose_new_bead` uses `source_occurrences` instead of counting findings: `TerminalReviewFinding::from_final_review` sets `source_occurrences` to `amendment.sources.len()` (line 1056), and `reconcile_proposed_bead_records` sums `source_occurrences` to compute `occurrence_count` (line 1407). A single final-review finding with multiple sources can therefore meet threshold 2+ and emit a proposal record, which violates the contract requirement to gate on the number of findings (case-insensitive summary equality), not source count.

`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1051` converts each `FinalReviewCanonicalAmendment` into exactly one terminal finding and drops its `sources`, then `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1403` uses that vector length for the proposal threshold. Final review deduplicates identical reviewer proposals while preserving the individual reviewer occurrences in `sources` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:681`). With the default threshold of 2, two reviewers proposing the same `proposed_bead_summary` collapse to one canonical amendment, `occurrence_count` becomes 1, and no `proposed-beads.ndjson` record is written even though the summary appeared in two findings.

Deferred-only review handling misses `ConditionallyApproved`. The remediation-trigger branch only handles `RequestChanges` with restart-triggering follow-ups (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5286`), the deferred-only no-remediation branch is also `RequestChanges`-only (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5473`), and `skip_next_apply_fixes_reason` only skips deferred-only `RequestChanges` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:8278`). A review stage that returns `ConditionallyApproved` with only `covered_by_existing_bead`, `propose_new_bead`, or `informational_only` findings falls through to normal next-stage advancement (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5649`), so QuickDev still enters `apply_fixes` and routes non-fix feedback through the implementer path.

In src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1313-1342, a covered_by_existing_bead finding that already has its amendment_id marker is supposed to be skipped, but the code increments existing_markers_seen and then calls sync_own_dirty_if_beads_healthy when existing_markers_seen > 0. A pure idempotent replay can therefore fail on unrelated pending br mutation state even though it posted no comment. Existing markers alone should not force a sync; only flush after posting a comment or when this adapter has its own recovered pending mutation.

In src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1386-1391, reconcile_proposed_bead_records reads proposed-beads.ndjson before it filters propose_new_bead groups and applies the threshold at 1393-1412. That means fix_current_bead/informational_only runs, or below-threshold proposals, can fail on an unrelated proposed-beads.ndjson I/O error even though those paths should be no-op/no-write. Build the proposal groups first and return before reading the file unless at least one group meets threshold.

src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:8217 now treats `classified_findings` as authoritative and returns at 8235-8237 whenever any classified item exists, so legacy `follow_up_or_amendments` are dropped instead of merged. Combined with the skip path at 8288-8294, a review payload containing one deferred classified finding plus an ordinary fix-now follow-up can skip `apply_fixes` and advance without remediation. This changes the `fix_current_bead` amendment flow; keep merging non-duplicate legacy follow-ups as fix-current items while filtering only non-fix classifications out of the immediate queue.

src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:484-490 treats a failed `br update --status=closed` as success if a follow-up read sees the bead closed, but src/adapters/br_process.rs:1335-1345 clears the pending mutation record on that failed update. In that false-failure case, `sync_after_close` has no durable pending record to flush, so the run can record success while the local bead close is never pushed. Restore a pending `update_bead_status(..., "closed")` record before accepting the observed closed state; mirror the same recovery in the CLI terminal close path at src/cli/run.rs:1220-1229.

src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1278-1283 accepts a `covered_by_existing_bead` target without checking whether it is the current `bead_id`, and 1320-1327 then posts the reconciliation comment to that target. If a reviewer accidentally points `covered_by_bead_id` or legacy `mapped_to_bead_id` at the active bead, this path modifies the current bead even though the contract says covered-by-existing must not modify it. Add a warn-and-skip guard when the normalized target matches the current bead.

The CLI terminal path still passes `project_id` as the `task_id` argument when invoking `reconcile_terminal_review_classifications` (and the downstream failure/success error path uses the same context), so all reconciliation diagnostics/sync error context is attached to the project instead of the concrete task execution. In `sync` logs this causes incorrect traceability and can make post-failure/operator forensics ambiguous when multiple tasks share a project/ bead context.

src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1301 only skips covered_by_existing_bead when the raw target string exactly equals the active bead_id, but the code then resolves the target with br show and posts the comment to detail.id at src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1346 without re-checking that the resolved bead is not the active bead. Because this repo supports short/dotted bead aliases elsewhere, a reviewer can provide an alias for the current bead that does not string-match bead_id, and reconciliation will still mutate the current bead, violating the explicit "Do NOT modify the current bead" requirement. Add a post-resolution guard against detail.id matching the active bead, ideally using the same canonical/alias matching semantics used by milestone bead refs, before checking comments or calling comment_bead.

In `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1446-1453`, `reconcile_proposed_bead_records` treats a summary bucket as already reconciled if it finds a single existing `amendment_id` match (`min()` of the group). That only suppresses duplicates when that exact representative ID was written before, so the same `proposed_bead_summary` can be written repeatedly across retries/runs when IDs differ (e.g., new payload run IDs). This causes duplicate entries in `proposed-beads.ndjson` even though proposal idempotency is intended at the handoff level.

In `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1064-1073` and `1459-1468`, `TerminalReviewFinding::from_final_review` converts a single final-review amendment into `occurrence_count = amendment.sources.len().max(1)`, and thresholding in `reconcile_proposed_bead_records` sums `occurrence_count` across findings. This makes one amendment with two sources count as 2 and cross the default threshold 2, causing a `proposed-beads.ndjson` record even though only one finding exists. The contract says to propose only when the same `proposed_bead_summary` appears in N or more findings (not sources), so this over-triggers at run time.

src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1168 records one TerminalReviewFinding per accepted canonical final-review amendment, then src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1457 applies the threshold to that count. Duplicate reviewer findings are merged into one canonical amendment with multiple sources in src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:681, so two reviewers proposing the same body/summary do not meet the default threshold of 2 and no proposed-beads.ndjson record is written.

src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1207 tracks existing summary_keys and src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:1483 skips any later threshold-met group with the same normalized summary. The idempotency requirement only says to skip records whose amendment_id is already present; this broader summary-level skip suppresses distinct later terminal runs or current beads with a new amendment_id but the same summary, so their source_run_id/current_bead_id/count handoff is never appended.

src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:8285 only skips ApplyFixes for an approved review when validation_follow_ups() is empty, but validation_follow_ups() includes informational_only, covered_by_existing_bead, and propose_new_bead classified findings from lines 8217-8233 even though those classifications are later filtered out of queued amendments. An Approved review that records only deferred or informational classified findings will therefore enter ApplyFixes with nothing current-bead-scoped to fix, violating the no-op/deferred routing contract. Extend the deferred-only skip at lines 8292-8298 to cover Approved reviews when there is no restart-triggering follow-up.
